title: andropogon sorghum subsp. verticilliflorus
intro: Andropogon sorghum subsp. verticilliflorus, also known as Indian millet or broom grass, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family and is native to tropical Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Andropogon sorghum subsp. verticilliflorus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
